question:
What is the main social critique of "respectability politics" — the idea that marginalized groups should conform to dominant cultural norms to earn equal treatment?
A) It is only critiqued in academic settings and has no practical relevance.
B) It places the burden of ending discrimination on its victims rather than on those perpetuating it.
C) Conforming to dominant norms has been proven to be completely ineffective at improving social outcomes.
D) It is a legal strategy that requires no critique because it is codified in civil rights law.
